summary: Iteration 2 — validated all new RPG mechanics: NPC memory + repeat-irritation, role-based dialogue (king refuses low-status, soldier threatens criminals, merchant scarcity hints), real economy with supply/demand (buy decreases supply + raises price; sell increases supply + drops price), 28-day passive simulation moves markets, attack_npc kills permanently with persistence in MongoDB and bounty for nobility, soldier_check enforcement on work/travel. Backend: 39 PASSED / 1 SKIPPED (skip is fixture-random — no commoner NPC at player's starting location in that run) across both regression and new feature suites. Frontend: confirmed Arz/Talep/Fiyat columns on City Detail (with red coloring for scarcity), HATIRLADIKLARI memory list on NPC Detail showing day/topic/delta entries, king NPC produced 3 DIFFERENT rejection lines from KING_REJECT pool on repeated selam chats (proves both faction-refuse and variation). SALDIR button correctly hidden when NPC not at player location (conditional render works as designed; backend attack flow already proven by pytest).
critical_code_review_comments: game_routes.py /chat sets npc['_loc_supply_signal'] as a transient field then pop()s it before save — works, but it mutates npc dict that's also written to Mongo via _save_state. The pop is BEFORE save so safe; just fragile if anyone reorders.

dialogue.py _refuse_audience for 'lord' fires only when reputation < -10 AND status < 3 — quite restrictive; most low-status players (köylü status=1, default rep=0) will NOT trigger lord rejection. Verify if intended.

game_routes.py attack_npc: when player loses, code sets player['health']=5 BEFORE the loss branch read it (line 374 vs 371). The order is OK because player_hp local is checked first, but the dual assignment is confusing.

Return shape inconsistency: /travel, /work, /crime, /attack_npc, /battle return {state, ...}, but /trade, /job, /quest/*, /marry, /advance, /new still return state directly. Frontend must handle both shapes consistently — recommend wrapping ALL responses in {state, ...}.

/api/game/work soldier_check only triggers when crime is high; the random nature means test cannot deterministically assert enforcement field has by/fine/days. Tests assert presence-of-key only, which is acceptable.
world_gen.py has SURNAMES with invisible zero-width char in 'Pa​şa' (carried over from iter1).

Frontend: NPCDetail SALDIR button visibility check uses 'npc.alive && npc.location_id === player.location_id'; this is correct but means players must first /travel to NPC's location — UX could surface a hint when both conditions aren't met.

success_rate.backend: 100% (39/39 passed, 1 skipped due to fixture-random NPC distribution; all 11 new-feature tests passed)
success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke for new features (Arz/Talep/Fiyat columns + scarcity coloring, HATIRLADIKLARI memory list, varied king rejection dialogue, SALDIR conditional rendering)

test_credentials: Self-registered users (feat_<uuid>@k.com / test123 for backend; ui2_<uuid>@k.com / test123 for UI). Pre-existing test@k.com / test123 still valid.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest module/run registers a fresh user and creates a procedural world via /api/game/new. NPC memory tests intentionally pump crime via repeated /crime calls to reach the >30 threshold for soldier threat tests. No persistent seed required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: BASE_URL is https://living-world-sim-4.preview.emergentagent.com (NOT kkm-rpg). Two backend test files now exist: test_kkm_api.py (regression, updated for new {state, enforcement} contract on /travel and /work) and test_kkm_new_features.py (memory, faction roles, supply/demand, attack_npc, enforcement). Run with: pytest backend/tests/ -v. Frontend testid namespaces: city-* on CityDetail, npc-* on NPCDetail (npc-attack for SALDIR, chat-topic-<id> for dialog topics, chat-log for log container). Memory entries render as 'Gün X: <topic> +N'. The SALDIR button only renders when the NPC location_id matches the player location_id — to test it via UI, first /travel the player to the NPC's location, OR test via the backend endpoint which already passes. Markets use loc['market'][good] = {price, supply, demand, base}; legacy loc['prices'] still mirrored for compatibility.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA. Two pre-existing regression tests (test_travel, test_work) failed initially because /travel and /work now return {state, enforcement, ...} instead of bare state — this is a documented contract change from iter1 to iter2. Updated both tests to unwrap body['state'] and they now pass.
